Video.summary: “The Century Race”: Dale believes he found the bike that will help him beat a rival in an annual race of 100 year old motorcycles. But will it run?
Video.title: The Century Race

Video.summary: A California collector gambles on a big payday from his Crocker/Caddy combo.
Video.title: Doubling Down

Video.summary: For Dale and Matt sometimes the dotted line to treasure splits and heads in opposite directions. In this edition of What’s in the Barn, time is thrust to the forefront of this multi-state, long distance, north, south search. Timeless and priceless 110 year old bikes, antique cars, airplanes, and the infamous Egberts 1930 neon Harley-Davidson sign that coined the phrase “Ride a Winner” are high on this father and son’s list of “must haves”. The clock ticks, both literally and figuratively, on one rare time piece and two old-timer’s cars, bikes, and automobiles.
Video.title: Time is Not On Their Side

Image.alt: Time is Not On Their Side
Time is Not On Their Side
Time is Not On Their Side

Video.summary: Promoters of the prestigious Amelia Island Concours have called on Dale to develop a show and field bikes for an “Orphan Bike” show. They have defined orphan bike as extremely rare, that aren't a Harley nor Indian machines.
Video.title: Orphan Bikes

Video.summary: “A Motorcycle Crime Story”: The horrific tale of a double murder over money and motorcycles.
Video.title: Motorcycle Crime Story

Video.summary: The RM Auction Company has worked with Dale before as a buyer, seller, historian, and caretaker at the prestigious Lee Hartung auction. But this event is no last minute rush to define one man’s treasures. This is a well calculated, time consuming, multi-media, and multi motorcycle and prized art deal.
Video.title: Second Time's a Charm

Video.summary: Home Depot Senior Vice President, Gordon Erickson, is a huge fan of Wheels Through Time. He is also a huge supporter of the Wounded Warrior program. For years the hardware giant has been donating goods and services to help returning servicemen with their fix-it-up projects. Now they would like to go one step further and build a WWII military bike to raise awareness and funds. Only problem is where to get one? Enter Dale and an exhaustive, time sensitive, cross country search for a rare military machine.
Video.title: Wounded Warrior Project

Video.summary: An family farm in the mid-west contains houses, barns, outbuildings, trailers, grain silos and buses full of old stuff and perhaps the biggest stash of undiscovered old motorcycles in the country. The collection was put together by their unnamed father. Seller agrees to have Dale broker rare Excelsior for high-end client. Dale has the buyer, but will he outbid his own client for the exclusive find?
Video.title: Diamond in Dodge City

Video.summary: Matt is out for revenge. Last year Dale beat him badly at the Annual Vintage bike race in Barber, Alabama. But this year there is a whole new tale to tell. With just days until the race - Dale is secretly struggling to get the bike he beat Matt with last year up and working. Matt struggles just to locate a 100-year old bike. Matt's prayers are answered thanks to an old Catholic Priest he located who has possession of a bike. The priest might let Matt have the bike to race - but there are conditions. The toughest one: Don't tell Dale you have it. Can Matt afford to buy the bike and get it running in time for the race without Dale seeing it? Will Dale even get his broken bike started? And if they ever get to race, will Matt exact his revenge?
Video.title: Don't Tell Dale

Video.summary: Built in the mid-60’s by eccentric engineer “Wild Bill” Gelbke, the Road Dog was, and still is, the largest motorcycle ever built. It utilized aircraft tubing, Corvette disc brakes, and a Chevy truck differential to keep it stable. The one-off creation disappeared when Gelbke did in 1978. Outflanked once when the bike was spotted and sold to a private collector in 1995, Dale has vowed to locate the machine and make Wheels Through Time its final resting place.
Video.title: Biker Lore

Video.summary: Big Time Motorcycle Auction
Video.title: Vegas Payday

Video.summary: “12hrs. & Running”: Dale turns a surprise 60th birthday party into a challenge to his guests to start 120 of the rarest motorcycles in the world.
Video.title: 12 Hours and Running

Video.summary: A cross-country race to lay claim to some of the world’s most sought after works specials.
Video.title: Hidden Hillclimbers

Video.summary: A vague tip leads Matt and Dale into a WWII era military warehouse and beyond.
Video.title: Peashooter

Video.summary: A thousand bulging barns of choppers, bobbers, and board trackers are emptied and on display in the Quad Cities.
Video.title: Davenport Swap

Video.summary: “Yes, Dale there is a 100 Year Old Harley”: Dale hopes his labor of love becomes a prized possession when a six-figure Harley goes to auction.
Video.title: 100 Year Old Harley

Video.summary: Nick Rutter, along with special guest Bruno Massel, walks you through step-by-step on how to perform a waterless wash, clay and polish, applying a sealant, and finishing with a topcoat wax on a brand-new car.
Video.title: Detailing a Brand New Car Part 1

Image.alt: Detailing a Brand New Car Part 1
Detailing a Brand New Car Part 1
Detailing a Brand New Car Part 1

Video.summary: Nick Rutter provides valuable tips and techniques for cleaning and rejuvenating your cars interior
Video.title: Interior Car Care

Video.summary: Kevin and Willie B take a 1970 Chevelle from an automatic to a manual transmission, outfitting the new engine along the way.
Video.title: Chevelle Part 1: Transmission Conversion

Video.summary: Everyone knows that with a classic beauty, it’s really what’s on the inside that counts. Willie B and Kevin finish up a 1970 Chevelle project with some much-needed attention to the interior.
Video.title: Chevelle Part 2: New Interior

Video.summary: Kevin and Willie beef up the suspension on a 2014 Jeep JK to transform it from a daily driver into a weekend off-roading vehicle.
Video.title: On-and-Offroad Jeep

Video.summary: A good diesel truck is indispensable on the jobsite, but Duramax diesels are known to have a few common problems. Kevin and Willie B show us what they are and how to fix them to keep your fleet complete.
Video.title: Duramax Diesel: Common Problems

Video.summary: Nice cars finish last. Willie B and Kevin turn a mellow 2002 Mustang GT into a mean, lean, power machine with a few performance upgrades.
Video.title: Meaner Mustang

Video.summary: Kevin and Willie diagnose a noise issue on a high-mileage F-150. Meanwhile, a battered old pickup shows up in the garage in need of serious repairs.
Video.title: A Tale of Two Trucks

Video.summary: Willie B. and Kevin go under the hood of the new Mustang EcoBoost 4-cylinder to add some performance upgrades. Meanwhile, progress on an old pickup project continues with rust repair and new body panels.
Video.title: EcoBoost Mustang

Video.summary: When a 1969 Camaro with bad body roll is delivered to the shop, Kevin and Willie tackle the suspension to help it perform better on the track.
Video.title: Tipsy Track Car

Video.summary: Mark and the Ghouls get down to work like never before in order to get the A100 ready for SEMA which is only weeks away. Brakes, suspension, tail lights, bed sealer, you name it and the Mopar Maniacs are racing to get it done. With time flying and stress mounting the team works long hours and weekends trying to finish this iconic build. With SEMA only days away, the A100 finally gets it’s iconic 426 HEMI engine, well, if they can make it fit!
Video.title: The A100 Team

Video.summary: There is no time left, SEMA is almost here but the iconic A100 still needs some buttoning up. Installing all of the final components is an all hands on deck task and when the dust settles, Mark has only the decals left to put on the car, completing its legendary look. With the A100 finished the team heads out to SEMA in order to roll the Little Dead Wagon over the red carpet for Mopar and Mark once again receives a very kind gift from our friends.
Video.title: Night of the Little Dead Wagon

Image.alt: Night of the Little Dead Wagon
Night of the Little Dead Wagon
Night of the Little Dead Wagon

Video.summary: When Mark get’s started talking about a car there is almost no stopping him. So it is again when Mark picks multiple years of a favorite Mopar of his and tells both the audience, and whoever will listen all about their amazing options and cool differences. The Ghouls join in on the fun just like last time to pick their favorite things as well. Don’t worry, though, he can’t talk forever right?
Video.title: Carbiturary: Cuda

Video.summary: With SEMA over the Ghoulz can finally rest and get back to work on all the cars they had to leave untouched for the last few weeks…again. First up on deck is our beautiful 1972 Duster. Needing its Dash installed as well as its interior there is still a lot of work left on this car! Additionally, metal work has begun on a host of new cars moving up through the shop, it never stops at Graveyard Carz.
Video.title: Duster In The Wind

Video.summary: A 1970 Challenger is a special thing, but a B5 Challenger is even more special. With the bumblebee stripe finally added to the car it’s time for the work to really begin. Work begins on all the things that make this 1970 Challenger special, including the headliner, dash, interior and more. With that done it’s time for this beautiful Challenger to get its 440 Six Pack!
Video.title: B5: Before & After

Video.summary: The time has finally come. Brett Torino is coming and he is here for his car, the legendary 1 of 2 ever made, 1970 Coronet! Mark wouldn’t be Mark, though, without a few tricks up his sleeve. When Brett arrives to collect on his long restoration from the Mopar Master, the reveal of this truly one of a kind car is done with style. But not before Mark takes us back through the entire restoration leading up to that point. It Is the last 1970 Hemi Coronet Convertible on earth, after all.
Video.title: The Last WS27R on Earth

Video.summary: The crew is overwhelmed with several restorations at once. From painting stripes, to engine parts, to missing parts on the 71 Charger, tensions are high around the shop. To Make things worse, Mark is obsessed with finding a factory pink Mopar for his granddaughter's 16th birthday.
Video.title: Emma's Car

Video.summary: The crew at Graveyard Carz are excited about the pumpkin carving contest, but when a mistake leaves the 'Cuda filled with water, the whole crew has to pay the price. Royal saves the day, but with the carving contest back on the table, where are the pumpkins? Mark has to swallow his pride and accept help from Daren for the contest to go ahead.
Video.title: It's the Great Pumpkin, Mark Worman!

Image.alt: It's the Great Pumpkin, Mark Worman!
It's the Great Pumpkin, Mark Worman!
It's the Great Pumpkin, Mark Worman!
